news.t: Philippine Central Bank Hikes Rates Amid Inflation Risks
Price caps, taking the stairs, and short-sleeved shirts: How countries are coping with the Iran war energy shock
Philippine Stocks Reclaim Pre-COVID High After Rate Cut

holdings.infoBox: EPHE is an equity ETF with a total of 41 individual holdings. The top holdings are INTERNATIONAL CONTAINER TERMINAL S stock at 24.61%, BDO UNIBANK INC at 10.46%, SM PRIME HOLDINGS INC at 6.04%, BANK OF THE PHILIPPINE ISLANDS at 4.81%, and SM INVESTMENTS CORP at 4.66%.

overview.description: The iShares MSCI Philippines ETF (EPHE) is an exchange-traded fund that is based on the MSCI Philippines IMI 25/50 index, a free float-adjusted market-cap-weighted index of the broader Filipino equity market. EPHE was launched on Sep 28, 2010 and is issued by BlackRock.

data.title: Philippine Central Bank Hikes Rates Amid Inflation Risks
data.text: Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas raised its benchmark rate to 4.75% in an attempt to tame inflation fueled by the conflict in the Middle East.

data.title: Price caps, taking the stairs, and short-sleeved shirts: How countries are coping with the Iran war energy shock
data.text: Countries around the world have scrambled to cope with the fallout of the energy shock from the Iran war. This comes as the Iran war stretches into its third week.

data.title: Philippine Stock Exchange: 'All bets are off' if the Middle East conflict continues indefinitely
data.text: Ramon Monzon of Philippines Stock Exchange discusses the recent impact of higher energy prices for Philippines' economy and markets. He also discusses its' IPO pipeline, including Maya Bank and Gcash.

data.title: Trump Sets Tariff on Philippines at 19%
data.text: President Donald Trump said the US reached an agreement with the Philippines setting a 19% tariff on the country's exports. Trump announced the deal on social media after meeting with Philippines Pres...

data.title: Philippine Stocks Reclaim Pre-COVID High After Rate Cut
data.text: Philippine stocks rocketed to a nine-month high after a shock interest rate cut. Gain exposure to the country's stocks with these ETFs.
